headlineSummary: Copilot drew the most negative reaction over trust and utility concerns, while Anthropic and OpenAI sat in a close tie with mixed reactions and Gemini had limited evidence [E2][E3][E10][E1][E6][E7][E8][E9][E11][E12].

ranking.summary: Copilot drew negative sentiment from trust concerns around commit attribution and from a user planning to cancel GitHub Copilot, with only one mild note of it catching a code issue [E2][E3][E4][E10].

Anthropic was mixed overall in a close tie with OpenAI, with praise for Claude Code and Claude work mixed against complaints about salesy writing, wasteful token use, refusals, and high cost [E1][E5][E6][E7][E8][E9].

OpenAI was mixed overall in a close tie with Anthropic, with one user praising ChatGPT for UI prototyping and another reporting better resume results, offset by complaints about refusals and an account warning [E6][E7][E11].
Gemini had limited evidence, with one mixed comment that liked the rewrite result but noted exaggeration and that recruiters preferred the older CV [E12].

evidence.excerpt: He used Claude Code! What an incredible enabler of fun little side projects it's turning into.

annotations.rationale: Explicit praise for Claude Code as an 'incredible enabler' attributes strong positive sentiment to Anthropic.

evidence.excerpt: VS Code inserting 'Co-Authored-by Copilot' into commits regardless of usage

annotations.rationale: The title frames Copilot as being credited even when unused, implying a trust/integrity problem.

evidence.excerpt: Falsifying who authored a piece of code just to pump up AI usage stats is a huge breach of trust and it is disappointing to see Microsoft prioritize branding over the integrity of the developer's log.

annotations.rationale: In the Copilot commit-attribution story context, this is strong criticism of Microsoft/Copilot as deceptive and trust-breaking.

evidence.excerpt: The best part is that copilot commented on the PR saying that this doesn’t actually change the behaviour, creates inconsistency in the codebase and suggested reverting the change!

annotations.rationale: The comment credits Copilot with correctly spotting a code/configuration issue and recommending a revert, which is mild praise of its usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: The README is unnerving. Do people really see the Claude-salesman style of writing as something normal? On the other hand, I should be thanking Anthropic for making it so easy to spot, they might have done this intentionally.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly associates an off-putting 'Claude-salesman style' with Anthropic and criticizes it.

evidence.excerpt: To be fair I find the approach from claude design incredibly wasteful of tokens, and time-consuming since it needs to build a full website. Their website is also clearly vibe-coded and not homogeneous in style with the rest. ChatGPT image 2 is much better at protoyping uis, cheaper and faster.

annotations.rationale: Claude Design is criticized as wasteful, time-consuming, and poorly styled.
ChatGPT image 2 is explicitly described as better, cheaper, and faster for UI prototyping.

evidence.excerpt: The biggest differentiator for me: DeepSeek just does what I ask. I've tried using both GPT and Claude for reverse engineering recently, both refused. I even got a warning on my OpenAI account.

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports GPT refused the task and OpenAI issued an account warning, signaling strong dissatisfaction with OpenAI's restrictions.
Claude is explicitly criticized for refusing the reverse-engineering task.

evidence.excerpt: On Claude Opus I think (from past experience before price hikes) these two prompts alone would have burned somewhere between $9 to $13 easily with not much benefit.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly criticizes Claude Opus as expensive and not delivering commensurate benefit.

evidence.excerpt: for working with claude code work well enough for me that they can reliably* produce code that matches the plan/spec in a way they did not till December 2025.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude Code now reliably produces code matching the plan/spec, indicating improved performance.

evidence.excerpt: For me, this is a real alternative after I cancel my github copilot towards the end of the month..

annotations.rationale: Calling DeepSeek a real alternative because the user plans to cancel GitHub Copilot signals mild negative sentiment toward Copilot's value.

evidence.excerpt: Just for kicks, I asked ChatGPT to “Analyze my resume and give it a score for what percentage it was in” then I asked it to revise it to make it score as high as possible I still tweaked and fact checked it but after I started sending that out, I got a much higher hit rate than before

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports better response rates after using ChatGPT to revise a resume, indicating practical benefit.

evidence.excerpt: I asked Gemini to rewrite it. Took a lot of time, because Gemini loves to exaggerate everything, but I'm quite happy with the result. The first couple of recruiters I sent it to preferred my old 7 page CV.

annotations.rationale: The commenter expresses mixed sentiment: happy with Gemini's rewrite overall, but notes exaggeration and says recruiters preferred the older CV.
